items.title: [正面] This gacha game is very much trying to be a real g...
items.summary:
This gacha game is very much trying to be a real game and, for the most part, succeeds.

The (English) translation was based on ChatGPT; this has mostly been fixed. Most, but not all, of the main story content is human-translated at this time, and most of the weirdness involving item/card descriptions is cleaned up. It's clear they intend to translate all of the story content given time, so this is no longer a concern.

The gameplay is solid - basically slay the spire mechanics. I can't compare it to StS specifically but as a deckbuilder it's certainly good, with solid team building. Setting up a thousand stacks of counter when a boss is attacking 6 times at once feels great. Or hiding behind shields while you slowly poison them to death. Or temporarily stealing their attack bonus for one turn while you unleash your own multi-hit attack. Or petrifying them when they're about to unleash a powerful attack. There are plenty of options available.

The problem is that this is still a gacha, and that is at odds with actual team building. Say you want to make a poison team. You can't do that if you haven't pulled any poison characters. Even if you have one, you'll need supports (likely other poison characters that you may not have). Even if you have someone good, they might not be very useful without specialized equipment (another gacha). Even if you have someone good, they might not actually be good without enough duplicate copies. "Doll - Destruction" in particular, I'm told, is fairly middling until you get eight copies of her, and then she's insane.

The starting units are really good, and you get at least two free picks for any non-limited character, and the game does seem to be very generous with free gifts, so this isn't as bad as it could be. But it still prevents experimentation and trying stuff out on your own. When characters are gated behind significant time or real-money requirements, I'm not going to splurge on them - first I'd need to figure out if they're actually good or a junk unit, find out how they would interact with other characters I already have and my equipment, etc. This means checking out guides, not trying to figure it out myself. There is a preview option for every character but it only shows so much (and shows them at maximum strength...)

From what I've heard, this game came close to hitting an early EOS. I don't want that to happen and I wish the devs the best. But if it does happen, I sincerely hope they do not throw away all of their hard work and instead re-release the game as a standalone, with the gacha removed and other elements streamlined. I'd buy that in an instant.


Edit:
Having given this game many more hours, I'm more leery than before. The game is still good and has plenty of promise, an interesting story, great character designs and art, and is overall fun. But it's still a gacha game. It absolutely wants all of your money and your time. Beyond the immediate money/grinding issue, it also complicates the skill-based approach the game [i]mostly[/i] takes. Did I lose that battle because I played my cards wrong, or was I just underleveled? Did I lose because I brought a bad team, or did I just not have enough dupes to unlock the necessary abilities? Did I lose because I focused on the wrong stats, or did I lose because I didn't want to spend 20 hours endlessly rerolling covenant substats?

Single player grindless games don't have this issue: you lost and it's no one's fault but your own. It wasn't bad at first, but at higher levels there's a greater expectation that you'll invest a ton of time into wheel dupes and covenant re-rolling, and covenants in particular are such an unbearable slog that I just don't want to interact with them.

I still recommend checking out the game, but boy can it get painful.

items.title: [正面] Slay the spire meets gacha with really generous ra...
items.summary:
Slay the spire meets gacha with really generous rates (3% for SSR). Story is good but at the moment it is just a machine translation. However, first set of human translations for English will be coming in v2.3 coming out end of July 2025! Gives pulling currency like no tomorrow just got to keep playing consistently. 

Game is challenging and refreshing in a lot of ways. Rogue-like aspect really makes for a new and interesting experience every play through.
